#### 2.9.6.2.0-5 Physical Downlink Shared Channel Receive (PDSCH) NR DL Signal Quality Level (ID: 12583528)

**2.9.6.2.0-5.0-1**  (ID: `12583579`)

Physical Downlink Shared Channel Receive (PDSCH)
PDSCH transmissions using 2TX transmission with rank 1

---

**2.9.6.2.0-5.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `12583580`)

Physical Downlink Shared Channel Receive (PDSCH)
Measurement: NR DL Signal Quality Level  
NetAct name: PDSCH\_2TX\_RI\_01  
PI ID: M55301C09001  
Note: PM counter definitions can be found in NIDD as explained in [11192143](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_28iOyPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9): PM Counter Search in NIDD.

Update time:

5G-L2-PS shall update counter when DL data using rank 1 (2Tx transmission) is transmitted on PDSCH regardless of the aggregation level and all below conditions are met:

* 5G-L2-PS sets per grant rank as an IE numOfLayers =1 indicated in DlData\_PdschSendReq.PdschEncoderConfig from 5G-L1-UL ([11512895](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRs3VvvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
* 5G-L2-PS receives PsCell\_CellSetupReq from 5G-CP-RT with mimoConfigData structure (5G\_L2\_IF\_73\_replaced([10967202](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2UTTdPu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))):
* IE DlMimoMode=ClosedLoopSpatialMultiplexing\_2x2 OR
* IE DlMimoMode=OpenLoopSpatialMultiplexing\_2x2

Increment value: The sum of DL grants sent over the measurement period.

---

**2.9.6.2.0-5.0-2**  (ID: `12583582`)

Physical Downlink Shared Channel Receive (PDSCH)
PDSCH transmissions using 2TX transmission with rank 2

---

Physical Downlink Shared Channel Receive (PDSCH)
Measurement: NR DL Signal Quality Level  
NetAct name: PDSCH\_2TX\_RI\_02  
PI ID: M55301C09002  
Note: PM counter definitions can be found in NIDD as explained in [11192143](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_28iOyPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9): PM Counter Search in NIDD.

Update time:

5G-L2-PS shall update counter when DL data using rank 2 (2Tx transmission) is transmitted on PDSCH regardless of the aggregation level and all below conditions are met:

* 5G-L2-PS set per grant rank as an IE numOfLayers =2 indicated in DlData\_PdschSendReq.PdschEncoderConfig from 5G-L1-UL ([11512895](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRs3VvvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
* 5G-L2-PS receives PsCell\_CellSetupReq from 5G-CP-RT with mimoConfigData structure (5G\_L2\_IF\_73\_replaced([10967202](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2UTTdPu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))):
* IE DlMimoMode=ClosedLoopSpatialMultiplexing\_2x2 OR
* IE DlMimoMode=OpenLoopSpatialMultiplexing\_2x2

Increment value: The sum of DL grants sent on PDCCH over the measurement period.

NetAct name: PDSCH\_4TX\_RI\_02  
PI ID: M55301C10002  
Note: PM counter definitions can be found in NIDD as explained in [11192143](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_28iOyPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9): PM Counter Search in NIDD.

Update time:

5G-L2-PS shall update counter when DL data using rank 2 (4Tx transmission) is transmitted on PDSCH regardless of the aggregation level and all below conditions are met:

* 5G-L2-PS set per grant rank as an IE numOfLayers =2 indicated in DlData\_PdschSendReq.PdschEncoderConfig from 5G-L1-UL ([11512895](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRs3VvvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
* 5G-L2-PS receives PsCell\_CellSetupReq from 5G-CP-RT with mimoConfigData structure (5G\_L2\_IF\_73\_replaced([10967202](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2UTTdPu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))):
* IE DlMimoMode=ClosedLoopSpatialMultiplexing\_4x4or4x2 OR
* IE DlMimoMode=OpenLoopSpatialMultiplexing\_4x4or4x2

Increment value: The sum of DL grants sent over the measurement period.

**2.9.6.2.0-5.0-7**  (ID: `12583598`)

Physical Downlink Shared Channel Receive (PDSCH)
Wideband CQI Histogram when UE reports related to 256QAM table - CQI Level 0 ... 31

---

**2.9.6.2.0-5.0-7.0-1**  (ID: `12583599`)

Physical Downlink Shared Channel Receive (PDSCH)
Measurement: NR DL Signal Quality Level  
NetAct name: WB\_CQI\_256TBL\_QCI\_00 ... WB\_CQI\_256TBL\_CQI\_15  
PI ID: M55301C20020 ... M55301C20035  
Note: PM counter definitions can be found in NIDD as explained in [11192143](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_28iOyPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9): PM Counter Search in NIDD.

Update time:

5G-L2-PS shall update counters of wideband CQI Histogram for UE reports related to 256QAM table when all below conditions are met:

* 5G-L2-PS receives decoded 4 bits of CQI for:
* PUCCH: IE uciBits in UlData\_PucchReceiveRespPs received by L2-PS from 5G-L1\_UL (5G\_L1\_IF\_221\_replaced([11514182](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FR2BQvvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) OR
* PUSCH: IE uciCsiPart1Bits OR IE uciCsiPart2Bits in UlData\_PuschReceiveRespPs received by L2-PS from 5G-L1\_UL (5G\_L1\_IF\_207\_replaced([11513864](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRzlB_vxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))
* 5G-L2-PS receives PsUser\_UserSetupReq from 5G-CP-RT with PdschConfig structure (5G\_L2\_IF\_428\_replaced([10975561](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2VBsNfu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))):
* IE mcsTable=1: 256QAM MCS table is used for PDSCH

Note:

5G-L2-PS shall check if is in transient phase, if yes then L2PS shall wait for the ending of transient phase when PsUser\_RrcReconfCompletedReq is received.

Increment value:  The number of times a UE has reported each Channel Quality Indicator (CQI) value. CQI index=0 is in range and should be incremented.

Counters are incremented before WB-CQI Averaging (5G\_UP\_ALG\_3005\_replaced([11125595](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqvu4vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

---

**2.9.6.2.0-5.0-9.0-1**  (ID: `12649432`)

Physical Downlink Shared Channel Receive (PDSCH)
Measurement: NR DL Signal Quality Level  
NetAct name: NUM\_CQI\_0\_RANK1 … NUM\_CQI\_15\_RANK1  
PI ID: M55301C00032 … M55301C00047  
Note: PM counter definitions can be found in NIDD as explained in [11192143](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_nXESkPvPEe-AqvopbP1qhQ): PM Counter Search in NIDD.

[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a sequence diagram illustrating the process of reporting Channel Quality Indicator (CQI) for RANK 1 in a 5G network. The diagram shows interactions between a User Equipment (UE), 5G-L1-UL (Layer 1 Uplink), 5G-L1-DL (Layer 1 Downlink), 5G-L2-PS (Layer 2 Packet Switched), and 5G-CP-RT (Control Plane Real-Time).
The diagram depicts the following sequence of events:
1. **Configuration Request:** 5G-L2-PS sends a `PsCell_CellSetupReq` or `PsCell_CellReconfigurationReq` to 5G-CP-RT. This request includes `actRadioQualityMeasCount = "8"` which signifies CQI distribution per RANK.
2. **Data Transmission:**
\* 5G-L1-DL sends `DIData_PdschSendReqL1sw/PdschEncoderConfigL1sw` or `DIData_PdschSendReqLoner/PdschEncoderConfigLoner` (with `numOfLayers = 1`) to UE. This likely involves scheduling of PDSCH (Physical Downlink Shared Channel) data.
\* UE transmits `PD SCH (TB)` (Transport Block) to 5G-L1-DL.
3. **CSI Reporting:**
\* UE sends a `PUCCH (CSI report)` to 5G-L1-UL.
\* There's an alternative path where UE sends `UIData_PucchReceiveRespPsL1sw` or `UIData_PucchReceiveRespPsLoner` (containing `uciBits`) to 5G-L1-DL.
\* Following this, UE sends `UIData_PuschReceiveRespPs/PuschUciBitsResp` (containing `uciCsiPart1Bits OR uciCsiPart2Bits`) to 5G-L1-UL.
4. **CQI Decoding and Assignment:** 5G-L2-PS decodes the UCI (Uplink Control Information) bits for CQI information type and assigns RANK 1 in each slot.
5. **Counter Update:** The counters are updated by one for the number of times CQI 0 to 15 is reported for RANK 1. This is represented by `NUM_CQI_0_RANK1` and `NUM_CQI_15_RANK1`.
The title of the diagram is " ".
At the bottom right corner, there is a URL: `http://msc-generator.sourceforge.net v6.3.7`.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/12724\_E\_NUM\_CQI\_x\_RANK1.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

Update time: 5G-L2-PS shall update counters of number of times CQI is reported for RANK 1 when below conditions are met:

* 5G-L2-PS receives PsCell\_CellSetupReq OR PsCell\_CellReconfigurationReq from 5G-CP-RT with pmqapCounterConfig structure ([12519675](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_u36DQBkaEfC3upM2pE5-4w)):
* actRadioQualityMeasCount = "8" (CQI distribution per RANK)
* 5G-L2-PS decodes UCI bits from below messages to extract CSI report which included the Rank Indicator = 1:
* PUCCH: IE uciBits in UlData\_PucchReceiveRespPsL1sw ([11514182](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_R4LsIvvwEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) or UlData\_PucchReceiveRespPsLoner ([11514193](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_R_ZucPvwEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))  received by L2-PS from 5G-L1\_UL OR
* PUSCH: IE uciCsiPart1Bits OR IE uciCsiPart2Bits in UlData\_PuschReceiveRespPs\PuschUciBitsResp ([11513864](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_OkRrYPvwEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) by L2-PS from 5G-L1\_UL (11513864)

Increment value: The counters is updated by one for the number of times CQI 0 … 15 reported for RANK 1.

Link to the file: [12724\_E\_NUM\_CQI\_x\_RANK1.signalling](https://nokia.sharepoint.com/:u:/r/sites/5Gdoc/AS_Tech_Spec/Drawings%20and%20diagrams%20for%205G/User%20Plane/5G%20L2%20Entity%20Level/2%205G-L2-PS%20Component%20Requirements/2.8%20PM%20Counters/CB012724_E/12724_E_NUM_CQI_x_RANK1.signalling?csf=1&web=1&e=qzD0Zl)

[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a sequence diagram illustrating a communication flow related to Channel Quality Indicator (CQI) reporting in a 5G network. The diagram depicts interactions between a User Equipment (UE), 5G-L1-UL (Layer 1 Uplink), 5G-L1-DL (Layer 1 Downlink), 5G-L2-PS (Layer 2 Packet Switched), and 5G-CP-RT (Control Plane Real-Time).
The diagram shows the following sequence of events:
1. **UE to 5G-L1-DL:** A PD SCH (Transport Block) is sent.
2. **5G-L1-DL to 5G-L2-PS:** Two possible messages are sent:
\* `DIData_PdschSendReqL1sw/PdschEncoderConfigL1sw`
\* `DIData_PdschSendReqLoner/PdschEncoderConfigLoner`
Both messages can include `(numOfLayers = 1 or 2)`.
3. **5G-L2-PS to 5G-CP-RT:** A message `PsCell_CellSetupReq` or `PsCell_CellReconfigurationReq` is sent, which includes `(actRadioQualityMeasCount = "8" (CQI distribution per RANK))`.
4. **UE to 5G-L1-UL:** A PUCCH (Physical Uplink Control Channel) with a CSI (Channel State Information) report is sent.
5. **5G-L1-UL to UE:** An alternative flow is shown with two possible responses:
\* `UIData_PucchReceiveRespPsL1sw` or `UIData_PucchReceiveRespPsLoner` (uciBits)
\* `UIData_PuschReceiveRespPs/PuschUciBitsResp` (uciCsiPart1Bits OR uciCsiPart2Bits)
6. **5G-L2-PS:** A note indicates that `5G_L2-PS decodes UCI bits for CQI information type and assigns RANK2 in each slot`.
7. **General Note:** A final note states that `The counters is updated by one for the number of times CQI 0...15 reported for RANK2`, with the counters being `NUM_CQI_0_RANK1... NUM_CQI_15_RANK2`.
The title of the diagram is `<Number of time CQI 0 ... CQI15 is reported for RANK 2 <NUM_CQI_0_RANK1... NUM_CQI_15_RANK2>`. The bottom right corner contains the text `http://msc-generator.sourceforge.net v6.3.7`.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/12724\_E\_NUM\_CQI\_x\_RANK2.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

Update time: 5G-L2-PS shall update counters of number of times CQI is reported for RANK 2 when below conditions are met:

* 5G-L2-PS receives PsCell\_CellSetupReq OR PsCell\_CellReconfigurationReq from 5G-CP-RT with pmqapCounterConfig structure ([12519675](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_u36DQBkaEfC3upM2pE5-4w)):
* actRadioQualityMeasCount = "8" (CQI distribution per RANK)
* 5G-L2-PS decodes UCI bits from below messages to extract CSI report which included the Rank Indicator = 2:
* PUCCH: IE uciBits in UlData\_PucchReceiveRespPsL1sw ([11514182](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_R4LsIvvwEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) or UlData\_PucchReceiveRespPsLoner ([11514193](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_R_ZucPvwEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))  received by L2-PS from 5G-L1\_UL OR
* PUSCH: IE uciCsiPart1Bits OR IE uciCsiPart2Bits in UlData\_PuschReceiveRespPs\PuschUciBitsResp ([11513864](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_OkRrYPvwEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) by L2-PS from 5G-L1\_UL (11513864)

Increment value: The counters is updated by one for the number of times CQI 0 … 15 reported for RANK 2.

Link to the file: [12724\_E\_NUM\_CQI\_x\_RANK2.signalling](https://nokia.sharepoint.com/:u:/r/sites/5Gdoc/AS_Tech_Spec/Drawings%20and%20diagrams%20for%205G/User%20Plane/5G%20L2%20Entity%20Level/2%205G-L2-PS%20Component%20Requirements/2.8%20PM%20Counters/CB012724_E/12724_E_NUM_CQI_x_RANK2.signalling?csf=1&web=1&e=5cJOIM)

[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a sequence diagram illustrating a process related to 5G communication, specifically focusing on Channel Quality Indicator (CQI) reporting for different Ranks. The diagram depicts interactions between a User Equipment (UE), 5G-L1-UL (Layer 1 Uplink), 5G-L1-DL (Layer 1 Downlink), 5G-L2-PS (Layer 2 Packet Switched), and 5G-CP-RT (Control Plane Real Time).
The diagram shows the following sequence of events:
1. **PD SCH (TB)**: A data packet (TB - Transport Block) is sent from 5G-L1-DL to the UE.
2. **PUCCH (CSI report)**: The UE sends a PUCCH (Physical Uplink Control Channel) containing a CSI (Channel State Information) report to 5G-L1-UL.
3. **Alternative Paths for CSI Report Processing**:
\* **Alt**: This indicates an alternative flow.
\* **UIData\_PucchReceiveRespPsL1sw or UIData\_PucchReceiveRespPsLoner (uciBits)**: This message is sent from 5G-L1-UL to 5G-L2-PS, carrying UCI bits.
\* **UIData\_PuschReceiveRespPs/PuschUciBitsResp (uciCsiPart1Bits OR uciCsiPart2Bits)**: This message is sent from 5G-L1-UL to 5G-L2-PS, carrying UCI CSI part 1 or part 2 bits.
4. **5G-L2-PS Actions**:
\* **DIData\_PdschSendReqL1sw/PdschEncoderConfigL1sw or DIData\_PdschSendReqLoner/PdschEncoderConfigLoner (numOfLayers = 3)**: These messages are sent from 5G-L1-DL to 5G-L2-PS, likely related to PDSCH (Physical Downlink Shared Channel) configuration.
\* **PsCell\_CellSetupReq or PsCell\_CellReconfigurationReq (ctRadioQualityMeasCount = "8" (CQI distribution per RANK))**: This message is sent from 5G-L2-PS to 5G-CP-RT, containing cell setup or reconfiguration information, including a measurement count for CQI distribution per RANK.
\* **5G\_L2-PS decodes UCI bits for CQI information type and assigns RANK3 in each slot**: This is a descriptive box indicating that 5G-L2-PS processes the received UCI bits to determine CQI information and assign RANK3.
5. **Counter Update**:
\* **The counters is updated by one for the number of times CQI 0... 15 reported for RANK 3 NUM\_CQI\_0\_RANK1... NUM\_CQI\_15\_RANK3**: This box describes a counter mechanism that increments for each reported CQI value (0 to 15) for RANK 3, with specific counter names like NUM\_CQI\_0\_RANK1 and NUM\_CQI\_15\_RANK3.
The title of the diagram is " ".
At the bottom right corner, there is a URL: "http://msc-generator.sourceforge.net v6.3.7".[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/12724\_E\_NUM\_CQI\_x\_RANK3.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

Update time: 5G-L2-PS shall update counters of number of times CQI is reported for RANK 3 when below conditions are met:

* 5G-L2-PS receives PsCell\_CellSetupReq OR PsCell\_CellReconfigurationReq from 5G-CP-RT with pmqapCounterConfig structure ([12519675](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_u36DQBkaEfC3upM2pE5-4w)):
* actRadioQualityMeasCount = "8" (CQI distribution per RANK)
* 5G-L2-PS decodes UCI bits from below messages to extract CSI report which included the Rank Indicator = 3:
* PUCCH: IE uciBits in UlData\_PucchReceiveRespPsL1sw ([11514182](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_R4LsIvvwEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) or UlData\_PucchReceiveRespPsLoner ([11514193](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_R_ZucPvwEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))  received by L2-PS from 5G-L1\_UL OR
* PUSCH: IE uciCsiPart1Bits OR IE uciCsiPart2Bits in UlData\_PuschReceiveRespPs\PuschUciBitsResp ([11513864](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_OkRrYPvwEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) by L2-PS from 5G-L1\_UL (11513864)

Increment value: The counters is updated by one for the number of times CQI 0 … 15 reported for RANK 3.

Link to the file: [12724\_E\_NUM\_CQI\_x\_RANK3.signalling](https://nokia.sharepoint.com/:u:/r/sites/5Gdoc/AS_Tech_Spec/Drawings%20and%20diagrams%20for%205G/User%20Plane/5G%20L2%20Entity%20Level/2%205G-L2-PS%20Component%20Requirements/2.8%20PM%20Counters/CB012724_E/12724_E_NUM_CQI_x_RANK3.signalling?csf=1&web=1&e=tbBWDD)

PI ID: M55301C00080 … M55301C00095  
Note: PM counter definitions can be found in NIDD as explained in [11192143](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_nXESkPvPEe-AqvopbP1qhQ): PM Counter Search in NIDD.

[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a sequence diagram illustrating a communication flow related to CQI (Channel Quality Indicator) reporting in a 5G network. The diagram depicts interactions between a User Equipment (UE), 5G-L1-UL (Layer 1 Uplink), 5G-L1-DL (Layer 1 Downlink), 5G-L2-PS (Layer 2 Packet Switched), and 5G-CP-RT (Control Plane Real Time).
The diagram shows the following sequence of events:
1. **PD SCH (TB)**: A message labeled "PD SCH (TB)" is sent from 5G-L1-DL to the UE.
2. **PUCCH (CSI report)**: The UE sends a "PUCCH (CSI report)" to 5G-L1-UL.
3. **Alternative Paths**: An "Alt" block indicates alternative paths for processing the CSI report.
\* **UIData\_PucchReceiveRespPsL1sw or UIData\_PucchReceiveRespPsLoner (uciBits)**: This message is sent from 5G-L1-UL to 5G-L2-PS.
\* **UIData\_PuschReceiveRespPs/PuschUciBitsResp (uciCsiPart1Bits OR uciCsiPart2Bits)**: This message is sent from 5G-L1-UL to 5G-L2-PS.
4. **5G-L2-PS Processing**:
\* **DIData\_PdschSendReqL1sw/PdschEncoderConfigL1sw or DIData\_PdschSendReqLoner/PdschEncoderConfigLoner (numOfLayers = 4)**: These messages are sent from 5G-L1-DL to 5G-L2-PS.
\* **PsCell\_CellSetupReq or PsCell\_CellReconfigurationReq (actRadioQualityMeasCount = "8" (CQI distribution per RANK))**: These messages are sent from 5G-L2-PS to 5G-CP-RT.
\* **5G\_L2-PS decodes UCI bits for CQI information type and assigns RANK4 in each slot**: This is a processing step within 5G-L2-PS.
5. **Counter Update**:
\* **The counters is updated by one for the number of times CQI 0... 15 reported for RANK 4 NUM\_CQI\_0\_RANK1... NUM\_CQI\_15\_RANK4**: This indicates a counter mechanism for tracking CQI reports for different ranks.
The title of the diagram is " ". The bottom right corner contains a URL: "http://msc-generator.sourceforge.net v6.3.7".[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/12724\_E\_NUM\_CQI\_x\_RANK4.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

Update time: 5G-L2-PS shall update counters of number of times CQI is reported for RANK 4 when below conditions are met:

* 5G-L2-PS receives PsCell\_CellSetupReq OR PsCell\_CellReconfigurationReq from 5G-CP-RT with pmqapCounterConfig structure ([12519675](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_u36DQBkaEfC3upM2pE5-4w)):
* actRadioQualityMeasCount = "8" (CQI distribution per RANK)
* 5G-L2-PS decodes UCI bits from below messages to extract CSI report which included the Rank Indicator = 4:
* PUCCH: IE uciBits in UlData\_PucchReceiveRespPsL1sw ([11514182](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_R4LsIvvwEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) or UlData\_PucchReceiveRespPsLoner ([11514193](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_R_ZucPvwEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))  received by L2-PS from 5G-L1\_UL OR
* PUSCH: IE uciCsiPart1Bits OR IE uciCsiPart2Bits in UlData\_PuschReceiveRespPs\PuschUciBitsResp ([11513864](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_OkRrYPvwEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) by L2-PS from 5G-L1\_UL (11513864)

Increment value: The counters is updated by one for the number of times CQI 0 … 15 reported for RANK 4.

Link to the file: [12724\_E\_NUM\_CQI\_x\_RANK4.signalling](https://nokia.sharepoint.com/:u:/r/sites/5Gdoc/AS_Tech_Spec/Drawings%20and%20diagrams%20for%205G/User%20Plane/5G%20L2%20Entity%20Level/2%205G-L2-PS%20Component%20Requirements/2.8%20PM%20Counters/CB012724_E/12724_E_NUM_CQI_x_RANK4.signalling?csf=1&web=1&e=rbODD1)

#### 2.9.6.2.0-6 Physical Downlink Shared Channel Receive (PDSCH) NR Signal Quality Level 1024QAM MCS table (ID: 13133883)

**2.9.6.2.0-6.0-1**  (ID: `13133884`)

Physical Downlink Shared Channel Receive (PDSCH)
PDSCH transmissions in Rank1 with 1024QAM MCS table - Bin0 ... Bin4

---

**2.9.6.2.0-6.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `13133916`)

Physical Downlink Shared Channel Receive (PDSCH)
**Measurement: NR Signal Quality Level 1024QAM MCS table**

**NetAct name: PDSCH\_RANK1\_1024TBL\_BIN0 ... PDSCH\_RANK1\_1024TBL\_BIN4**

**PI ID: M55402C00001 ... M55402C00005**

**Note: PM counter definitions can be found in NIDD as explained in [11192143: PM Counter Search in NIDD](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_28iOyPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Frm%2Fcm%2Fstream%2F_5KBG5p5DEe2A-5U7tzedRA).**

**Update time:**

Update time: 5G-L2-PS shall update the counter when DL transmission with numOfLayers = 1 is transmitted over PDSCH using 1024QAM MCS table.

**Increment value:**

5G-L2-PS shall increment dedicate counter according to the modulation order used for PDSCH scheduling:

* PDSCH\_RANK1\_1024TBL\_BIN0: QPSK (corresponding MCS indexes: 0, 1, 2, 27)
* PDSCH\_RANK1\_1024TBL\_BIN1: 16Qam (corresponding MCS indexes: 3, 4, 5, 28)
* PDSCH\_RANK1\_1024TBL\_BIN2: 64Qam (corresponding MCS indexes: 6, 7, 8, 9, 10, 11, 12, 13, 14, 29)
* PDSCH\_RANK1\_1024TBL\_BIN3: 256Qam (corresponding MCS indexes: 15, 16, 17, 18, 19, 20, 21, 22, 30)
* PDSCH\_RANK1\_1024TBL\_BIN4: 1024Qam (corresponding MCS indexes: 23, 24, 25, 26, 31)

**2.9.6.2.0-6.0-5**  (ID: `13133886`)

Physical Downlink Shared Channel Receive (PDSCH)
Wideband CQI Histogram when UE reports related to 1024QAM table - CQI Level 0 ... 15

---

**2.9.6.2.0-6.0-5.0-1**  (ID: `13133921`)

Physical Downlink Shared Channel Receive (PDSCH)
**Measurement: NR Signal Quality Level 1024QAM MCS table**

**NetAct name: WB\_CQI\_1024TBL\_CQI\_00 ... WB\_CQI\_1024TBL\_CQI\_15**

**PI ID: M55402C00021 ... M55402C00036**

**Note: PM counter definitions can be found in NIDD as explained in [11192143: PM Counter Search in NIDD](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_28iOyPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Frm%2Fcm%2Fstream%2F_5KBG5p5DEe2A-5U7tzedRA).**

Update time:

5G-L2-PS shall update counters of wideband CQI Histogram for UE reports related to 1024QAM table when all below conditions are met:

* 5G-L2-PS receives decoded 4 bits of CQI for:
* PUCCH: IE uciBits in UlData\_PucchReceiveRespPsL1sw/UlData\_PucchReceiveRespPsLoner received by L2-PS from 5G-L1\_UL ([11514174](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FR2BQPvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)), or
* PUSCH: IE uciCsiPart1Bits or IE uciCsiPart2Bits in UlData\_PuschReceiveRespPs received by L2-PS from 5G-L1\_UL ([11513831](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRzlAPvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9))
* 5G-L2-PS receives PsUser\_UserSetupReq or PsUser\_UserModifyReq from 5G-CP-RT with PdschConfig structure (5G\_L2\_IF\_428\_replaced([10975561](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2VBsNfu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))):
* IE mcsTable=3: 1024QAM MCS table is used for PDSCH

Note:

5G-L2-PS shall check if is in transient phase, if yes then L2PS shall wait for the ending of transient phase when PsUser\_RrcReconfCompletedReq is received.

Increment value:  The number of times a UE has reported each Channel Quality Indicator (CQI) value. CQI index=0 is in range and should be incremented.

Counters are incremented before WB-CQI Averaging (5G\_UP\_ALG\_3005\_replaced([11125595](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqvu4vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

---

Physical Downlink Shared Channel Receive (PDSCH)
VoNR UE Wideband CQI Histogram when UE reports related to 1024QAM table - CQI Level 0 ... 15

---

**2.9.6.2.0-6.0-6.0-1**  (ID: `13133922`)

Physical Downlink Shared Channel Receive (PDSCH)
**Measurement: NR Signal Quality Level 1024QAM MCS table**

**NetAct name: WB\_CQI\_1024TBL\_5QI1\_CQI\_00 ... WB\_CQI\_1024TBL\_5QI1\_CQI\_15**

**PI ID: M55402C00037 ... M55402C00052**

**Note: PM counter definitions can be found in NIDD as explained in [11192143: PM Counter Search in NIDD](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_28iOyPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Frm%2Fcm%2Fstream%2F_5KBG5p5DEe2A-5U7tzedRA).**

Update time:

5G-L2-PS shall update counters of wideband CQI Histogram for VoNR UE reports related to 1024QAM table when all below conditions are met:

* 5G-L2-PS receives decoded 4 bits of CQI for:
* PUCCH: IE uciBits in UlData\_PucchReceiveRespPsL1sw/UlData\_PucchReceiveRespPsLoner received by L2-PS from 5G-L1\_UL ([11514174](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FR2BQPvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)), or
* PUSCH: IE uciCsiPart1Bits or IE uciCsiPart2Bits in UlData\_PuschReceiveRespPs received by L2-PS from 5G-L1\_UL ([11513831](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRzlAPvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9))
* 5G-L2-PS receives PsUser\_UserSetupReq or PsUser\_UserModifyReq from 5G-CP-RT with PdschConfig structure (5G\_L2\_IF\_428\_replaced([10975561](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2VBsNfu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))):
* IE mcsTable=3: 1024QAM MCS table is used for PDSCH
* ​​​​​​​the UE has a voice (5QI1) bearer

Note:

5G-L2-PS shall check if is in transient phase, if yes then L2PS shall wait for the ending of transient phase when PsUser\_RrcReconfCompletedReq is received.

Increment value:  The number of times a UE has reported each Channel Quality Indicator (CQI) value. CQI index=0 is in range and should be incremented.

Counters are incremented before WB-CQI Averaging (5G\_UP\_ALG\_3005\_replaced([11125595](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqvu4vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

#### 2.9.6.2.0-7 Physical Downlink Shared Channel Receive (PDSCH) NR MAC PDU throughput (ID: 12583521)

**2.9.6.2.0-7.0-1**  (ID: `12583557`)

Physical Downlink Shared Channel Receive (PDSCH)
Initial and retransmission volume on PDSCH with 64QAM MCS table using MCS 0 ... MCS 31

---

**2.9.6.2.0-7.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `12583574`)

Physical Downlink Shared Channel Receive (PDSCH)
Measurement: NR MAC PDU throughput  
NetAct name: PDSCH\_INIRE\_VOL\_64TBL\_MCS00 ... PDSCH\_INIRE\_VOL\_64TBL\_MCS31      
PI ID: M55304C02000 ... M55304C02031  
Note: PM counter definitions can be found in NIDD as explained in [11192143](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_28iOyPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9): PM Counter Search in NIDD.

Update time:

5G-L2-PS shall update counters when DL data is scheduled over PDSCH with CP-OFDM and 64QAM MCS table so when all below conditions are met:

* 5G-L2-PS receives PsUser\_UserSetupReq from 5G-CP-RT with PdschConfig structure (5G\_L2\_IF\_428\_replaced([10975561](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2VBsNfu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))):
* IE mcsTable=0: 64QAM table

Increment value: The number of bytes (i.e. including padding) on transport blocks scheduled for initial transmission or retransmission.

Note:

1. For DL is used CP-OFDM only.

2. This counter is also updated when control message (SRB data, RA Resp (msg2), RA msg4) is scheduled over PDSCH.

---

**2.9.6.2.0-7.0-3.0-1**  (ID: `13133914`)

Physical Downlink Shared Channel Receive (PDSCH)
**Measurement: NR DL HARQ 1024QAM MCS table**

**NetAct name: PDSCH\_INI\_VOL\_1024TBL\_BIN0 ... PDSCH\_INI\_VOL\_1024TBL\_BIN4**

**PI ID: M55401C00124 ... M55401C00128**

**Note: PM counter definitions can be found in NIDD as explained in [11192143: PM Counter Search in NIDD](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_28iOyPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Frm%2Fcm%2Fstream%2F_5KBG5p5DEe2A-5U7tzedRA).**

**Update time:**

Update time: 5G-L2-PS shall update the counter when DL data is scheduled over PDSCH sent as initial transmission using 1024QAM MCS table.

**Increment value:**

The number of bytes (i.e. including padding) on transport blocks scheduled for initial transmission .

NOTE. This counter is also updated when control message (SRB data) is scheduled over PDSCH.

5G-L2-PS shall increment dedicate counter according to the modulation order used for PDSCH scheduling:

* PDSCH\_INI\_VOL\_1024TBL\_BIN0: QPSK (corresponding MCS indexes: 0, 1, 2, 27)
* PDSCH\_INI\_VOL\_1024TBL\_BIN1: 16Qam (corresponding MCS indexes: 3, 4, 5, 28)
* PDSCH\_INI\_VOL\_1024TBL\_BIN2: 64Qam (corresponding MCS indexes: 6, 7, 8, 9, 10, 11, 12, 13, 14, 29)
* PDSCH\_INI\_VOL\_1024TBL\_BIN3: 256Qam (corresponding MCS indexes: 15, 16, 17, 18, 19, 20, 21, 22, 30)
* PDSCH\_INI\_VOL\_1024TBL\_BIN4: 1024Qam (corresponding MCS indexes: 23, 24, 25, 26, 31)

Physical Downlink Shared Channel Receive (PDSCH)
**Measurement: NR DL HARQ 1024QAM MCS table**

**Note: PM counter definitions can be found in NIDD as explained in [11192143: PM Counter Search in NIDD](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_28iOyPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Frm%2Fcm%2Fstream%2F_5KBG5p5DEe2A-5U7tzedRA).**

Update time: 5G-L2-PS shall update the counter when DL data is scheduled over PDSCH sent using 1024QAM MCS table.

The number of bytes (i.e. including padding) on transport blocks scheduled for initial transmission or retransmission.

5G-L2-PS shall increment dedicate counter according to the modulation order used for PDSCH scheduling:

* PDSCH\_INRE\_VOL\_1024TBL\_BIN0: QPSK (corresponding MCS indexes: 0, 1, 2, 27)
* PDSCH\_INRE\_VOL\_1024TBL\_BIN1: 16Qam (corresponding MCS indexes: 3, 4, 5, 28)
* PDSCH\_INRE\_VOL\_1024TBL\_BIN2: 64Qam (corresponding MCS indexes: 6, 7, 8, 9, 10, 11, 12, 13, 14, 29)
* PDSCH\_INRE\_VOL\_1024TBL\_BIN3: 256Qam (corresponding MCS indexes: 15, 16, 17, 18, 19, 20, 21, 22, 30)
* PDSCH\_INRE\_VOL\_1024TBL\_BIN4: 1024Qam (corresponding MCS indexes: 23, 24, 25, 26, 31)

---

### 2.9.6.3 PM Counters for Active UEs with data in DL buffer

#### 2.9.6.3.0-1 PM Counters for Active UEs with data in DL buffer (ID: 11197493)

**2.9.6.3.0-1.0-3**  (ID: `11197505`)

PM Counters for Active UEs with data in DL buffer
[BEFORE CNI-127108]

Upon reception of a "LoCtrl\_DlBufferStatusInd message (according to 5G\_L2\_IF\_869\_replaced([10984606](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2WBKwfu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))) by L2-PS from L2-LO as a triggering event, the PM Counters of class 'NR Cell Utilization' related to the 'Number of Active UEs with data in DL buffer' shall be processed.

* With each arrival of ‘LoCtrl\_DlBufferStatusInd’ message the individual UEs with data in DL buffer in at least one of their DRB at this specific time slot, shall be determined and shall be processed internally in the following way:
* actual (bit) array for all attached UEs: The correlated bit for the UE is set to TRUE in the case the UE is indicated to have data in DL buffer. In the case the UE is not mentioned in the message the correlated bit for this UE is set to FALSE. The array contains for each UE the last actual DL buffer status information.
* max\_UE\_DL: The actual (bit) array for all attached UEs is scanned and the actual number of UE having data in DL buffer at this time in determined. In the case that this value is larger than the current maximum number of UEs that have data in DL buffer 'max\_UE\_DL' value is replaced by this actual value.
* sampling period (bit) array for all attached UE: The correlated bit for a UE is set to TRUE in the case that the bit for this UE was set from FALSE to TRUE in the actual (bit) array. The correlated bit for a UE is not set to FALSE in the case that the bit for this UE was set from TRUE to FALSE in the actual bit array. The array contains for each UE the information whether the UE had data in DL buffer during the sampling period.

In the case that in the next time slot no such message arrives, it shall be assumed that there was no change in terms of buffer status.

 At the end of the sampling period when the rdPmCounter523FSamplingPeriod timer expires, the arrays and the following PM counters shall be processed:

* the current sample value will be set to the number of UEs having bits set in the sampling period (bit) array.

Then the sampling period (bit) array will be set to the actual (bit) array for all attached UEs in order to start the new sampling period monitoring with the latest value.

* M55308C02000, aka ACC\_UE\_DL\_DRB\_DATA, indicates the accumulated number (sum over samples) of active UEs having data in DL buffer for at least one of their DRBs in the related P- or S-subcell. The current sample value is added to the old PM counter value of ACC\_UE\_DL\_DRB\_DATA:

ACC\_UE\_DL\_DRB\_DATA = ACC\_UE\_DL\_DRB\_DATA + current sample value

* M55308C02001, aka MAX\_UE\_DL\_DRB\_DATA, indicates the actual maximum number (maximum over samples) of active UEs having data in DL buffer in the related subcell inside the running sampling period. The current max\_UE\_DL value is checked against the old value of MAX\_UE\_DL\_DRB\_DATA. If the max\_UE\_DL value is larger than the previous value, the max\_UE\_DL value shall become the new PM counter sample value, otherwise the previous maximum value shall become the PM counter sample value.

If (max\_UE\_DL > MAX\_UE\_DL\_DRB\_DATA ) {

MAX\_UE\_DL\_DR\_DATA = max\_UE\_DL;

}

Else {}

* M55308C01000, aka SAMPLE\_CNT\_NR\_CELL\_UTIL\_DL describes the number of samples taken to the ACC – counter.

So the actual value of SAMPLE\_CNT\_NR\_CELL\_UTIL\_DL shall be incremented after each sample period:

SAMPLE\_CNT\_NR\_CELL\_UTIL\_DL = SAMPLE\_CNT\_NR\_CELL\_UTIL\_DL + 1

Note:

 At the end of the reporting period the sample Counters shall be given to the PM counter framework. From  ACC\_UE\_DL\_DRB\_DATA/SAMPLE\_CNT\_NR\_CELL\_UTIL\_DL an average UE number per sampling period can directly be calculated by higher layers.

By decreasing the length of the sampling period ideally to the length of 1 slot, the Average number of UEs that have data in DL buffer per slot can be determined.

 All cells that are configured for the UE shall get the same counter values assigned.

 PM counter M55308C33000 ( ACC\_UE\_MICRO\_DTX\_OPTIMIZED) for Micro-DTX feature is specified in 5G\_L2\_5905\_replaced([11192263](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_28i12PvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for the reader convenience.

[CNI-127108]

Measurement: NR Cell Utilization

NetAct name: ACC\_UE\_DL\_DRB\_DATA

PI ID: M55308C02000

Note: PM counter definitions can be found in NIDD as explained in 5G\_L2\_13022\_replaced([11192143](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_28iOyPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Update time:

Upon reception of a "LoCtrl\_DlBufferStatusInd message (according to 5G\_L2\_IF\_869\_replaced([10984606](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2WBKwfu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))) by L2-PS from L2-LO as a triggering event, the PM Counters of class 'NR Cell Utilization' related to the 'Number of Active UEs with data in DL buffer' shall be processed.

* With each arrival of ‘LoCtrl\_DlBufferStatusInd’ message the individual UEs with data in DL buffer in at least one of their DRB at this specific time slot, shall be determined and shall be processed internally in the following way:
* actual (bit) array for all attached UEs: The correlated bit for the UE is set to TRUE in the case the UE is indicated to have data in DL buffer. In the case the UE is not mentioned in the message the correlated bit for this UE is set to FALSE. The array contains for each UE the last actual DL buffer status information.
* max\_UE\_DL: The actual (bit) array for all attached UEs is scanned and the actual number of UE having data in DL buffer at this time in determined. In the case that this value is larger than the current maximum number of UEs that have data in DL buffer 'max\_UE\_DL' value is replaced by this actual value.
* sampling period (bit) array for all attached UE: The correlated bit for a UE is set to TRUE in the case that the bit for this UE was set from FALSE to TRUE in the actual (bit) array. The correlated bit for a UE is not set to FALSE in the case that the bit for this UE was set from TRUE to FALSE in the actual bit array. The array contains for each UE the information whether the UE had data in DL buffer during the sampling period.

In the case that in the next time slot no such message arrives, it shall be assumed that there was no change in terms of buffer status.

 At the end of the sampling period when the rdPmCounter523FSamplingPeriod timer expires, the arrays and the following PM counters shall be processed:

* the current sample value will be set to the number of UEs having bits set in the sampling period (bit) array.

Then the sampling period (bit) array will be set to the actual (bit) array for all attached UEs in order to start the new sampling period monitoring with the latest value.

Increment value:

* M55308C02000, aka ACC\_UE\_DL\_DRB\_DATA, indicates the accumulated number (sum over samples) of active UEs having data in DL buffer for at least one of their DRBs in the related P- or S-subcell. The current sample value is added to the old PM counter value of ACC\_UE\_DL\_DRB\_DATA:

ACC\_UE\_DL\_DRB\_DATA = ACC\_UE\_DL\_DRB\_DATA + current sample value.

Note:

 At the end of the reporting period the sample Counters shall be given to the PM counter framework. From  ACC\_UE\_DL\_DRB\_DATA/SAMPLE\_CNT\_NR\_CELL\_UTIL\_DL an average UE number per sampling period can directly be calculated by higher layers.

By decreasing the length of the sampling period ideally to the length of 1 slot, the Average number of UEs that have data in DL buffer per slot can be determined.

 All cells that are configured for the UE shall get the same counter values assigned.

 PM counter M55308C33000 ( ACC\_UE\_MICRO\_DTX\_OPTIMIZED) for Micro-DTX feature is specified in 5G\_L2\_5905\_replaced([11192263](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_28i12PvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for the reader convenience.

[END CNI-127108]

Update time: same as M55308C02000 (5G\_L2\_346\_replaced([11197493](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_29MWGfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) update time.

Increment value:

[END CNI-127108]

Counters ACC\_UE\_DL\_DRB\_DATA\_PQMQAP(M55356C00000)/MAX\_UE\_DL\_DRB\_DATA\_PMQAP(M55356C00002) are based on M55308C02000(ACC\_UE\_DL\_DRB\_DATA)/M55308C02001(MAX\_UE\_DL\_DRB\_DATA), 5G\_L2\_346\_replaced([11197493](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_29MWGfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)): PM Counters for Active UEs with data in DL buffer. That the UEs are grouped by each PMQAP profile.

Note: 1. logicalChannelId can be used to determine whether the UE's buffer is empty with its qosId.

2. If the UE with DL DRB data belongs to more than one PMQAP profiles, then each value related to the UE should be accumulate.

3. If PMQAP profiles online changed, then keep the old the UE's PMQAP profile index unchanged until the UE or the bear released, but applied for the new UE or bear attached.

#### 2.9.6.3.0-8 PM Counters for Active UEs with data in DL buffer Accumulated DRX sleep state UEs with DL DRB data buffered (ID: 11197568)

**2.9.6.3.0-8.0-2**  (ID: `11197576`)

PM Counters for Active UEs with data in DL buffer
[Before CNI-127108]

For 'NR Cell Utilization' class, according to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_6272\_replaced([11230269](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kytYEPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), PM Counter M55308C27001 (ACC\_UE\_DL\_SLEEP\_DATA) shall be incremented each slot by the number of UEs which have any DL data buffered in any DRB while being in DRX sleep state. Buffered DL SRB data is not counted.

The following implementation is proposed:

5G-L2-PS maintains the DRX (sleep) status (bit) array for all attached UEs: The correlated bit for a specific UE is set to TRUE in case UE which has been configured with DRX config enters DRX sleep state. The correlated bit for specific UE is set to FALSE in following cases:

 UE which has been configured with DRX config enters DRX active state

 UE is initially added to the array (bit for a UE which has not been configured with DRX config is then permanently kept as FALSE).

It is recommended to maintain the UE keeping order from actual (bit) array for all attached UEs indicating data present in DL buffer defined at 5G\_L2\_346\_replaced([11197493](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_29MWGfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), to be able to easily access corresponding information for the UEs between the (bit) arrays.

With actual (bit) array for all attached UEs indicating data present in DL buffer defined at 5G\_L2\_346\_replaced([11197493](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_29MWGfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) referred as 'DL buffer array' and DRX (sleep) status (bit) array for all attached UEs referred as 'DRX array':

 ACC\_UE\_DL\_SLEEP\_DATA is incremented each slot by the number of UEs that have corresponding entries set to TRUE in both DL buffer and DRX arrays.

DRX Config presence for the UE can be determined by interpretation of structure drxConfig (see 5G\_L2\_IF\_426\_replaced([10975509](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2VBFLvu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))) in PsUser\_UserSetupReq (see 5G\_L2\_IF\_389\_replaced([10974645](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2U9ayPu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and PsUser\_UserModifyReq (see 5G\_L2\_IF\_453\_replaced([10976074](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2VEvgvu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

For UE DRX status update reference, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2261\_replaced([11114317](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpkDI_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Note: Since for already existing 'NR Cell Utilization' class DL DRB buffer counters sampling period can be configured to a value greater than one slot, counter ACC\_UE\_DL\_SLEEP\_DATA can operate only on actual (bit) array (instead of sampling period (bit) array). Despite actual (bit) array usage, ACC\_UE\_DL\_SLEEP\_DATA can be updated in Post Scheduling (in contrary to ACC\_CELL\_DL\_DRB\_DATA, see 5G\_L2\_2995\_replaced([11192249](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_28i11fvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) alongside ACC\_UE\_DL\_DRB\_DATA (see 5G\_L2\_346\_replaced([11197493](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_29MWGfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), since DL buffer of UEs in DRX sleep state will not be altered due to the scheduling process.

[CNI-127108]

Measurement: NR Cell Utilization

NetAct name: ACC\_UE\_DL\_SLEEP\_DATA

PI ID: M55308C27001

Note: PM counter definitions can be found in NIDD as explained in 5G\_L2\_13022\_replaced([11192143](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_28iOyPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Update time: shall be incremented each slot by the number of UEs which have any DL data buffered in any DRB while being in DRX sleep state. Buffered DL SRB data is not counted.

Increment value :

The following implementation is proposed:

5G-L2-PS maintains the DRX (sleep) status (bit) array for all attached UEs: The correlated bit for a specific UE is set to TRUE in case UE which has been configured with DRX config enters DRX sleep state. The correlated bit for specific UE is set to FALSE in following cases:

 UE which has been configured with DRX config enters DRX active state

 UE is initially added to the array (bit for a UE which has not been configured with DRX config is then permanently kept as FALSE).

It is recommended to maintain the UE keeping order from actual (bit) array for all attached UEs indicating data present in DL buffer defined at 5G\_L2\_346\_replaced([11197493](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_29MWGfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), to be able to easily access corresponding information for the UEs between the (bit) arrays.

With actual (bit) array for all attached UEs indicating data present in DL buffer defined at 5G\_L2\_346\_replaced([11197493](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_29MWGfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) referred as 'DL buffer array' and DRX (sleep) status (bit) array for all attached UEs referred as 'DRX array':

 ACC\_UE\_DL\_SLEEP\_DATA is incremented each slot by the number of UEs that have corresponding entries set to TRUE in both DL buffer and DRX arrays.

DRX Config presence for the UE can be determined by interpretation of structure drxConfig (see 5G\_L2\_IF\_426\_replaced([10975509](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2VBFLvu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))) in PsUser\_UserSetupReq (see 5G\_L2\_IF\_389\_replaced([10974645](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2U9ayPu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and PsUser\_UserModifyReq (see 5G\_L2\_IF\_453\_replaced([10976074](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2VEvgvu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

For UE DRX status update reference, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2261\_replaced([11114317](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpkDI_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Note: Since for already existing 'NR Cell Utilization' class DL DRB buffer counters sampling period can be configured to a value greater than one slot, counter ACC\_UE\_DL\_SLEEP\_DATA can operate only on actual (bit) array (instead of sampling period (bit) array). Despite actual (bit) array usage, ACC\_UE\_DL\_SLEEP\_DATA can be updated in Post Scheduling (in contrary to ACC\_CELL\_DL\_DRB\_DATA, see 5G\_L2\_2995\_replaced([11192249](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_28i11fvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) alongside ACC\_UE\_DL\_DRB\_DATA (see 5G\_L2\_346\_replaced([11197493](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_29MWGfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), since DL buffer of UEs in DRX sleep state will not be altered due to the scheduling process.

[End CNI-127108]

## 2.9.7 Load Control

### 2.9.7.0-2 Load Control Number of skipped periodic SRS processing for wideband BF (ID: 11197588)

**2.9.7.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11197591`)

Load Control
Measurement: NR SRS Quality

NetAct name: NUM\_P\_SRS\_PROC\_SKIPPED

PI ID: M55371CRD50001

Note: PM counter definitions can be found in NIDD as explained in 5G\_L2\_13022\_replaced([11192143](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_28iOyPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Update time:

When scheduling of UlData\_SrsReceiveReq ->srsBmStruct with ignoreBM=True.

Increment value:

This counter is incremented when a periodic SRS is skipped from processing.

Note 1: The counter is incremented for each used Cyclic Shift (CS) of P-SRS resource when skipped.

---

### 2.9.7.0-3 Load Control SRS BM Resp Event discard (ID: 11197596)

**2.9.7.0-3.0-1**  (ID: `11197599`)

Load Control
Measurement: NR Beamforming 2

NetAct name: BM\_SRS\_RESP\_DISCARD

PI ID: M55330CRD50017

Note: PM counter definitions can be found in NIDD as explained in 5G\_L2\_13022\_replaced([11192143](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_28iOyPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Update time:

When storing or processing SrsReceiveRespBmPs message.

Increment value:

Increment 1 when one SrsReceiveRespBmPs messages discarded because of aging.

Note: Any messages discarded from the FIFO queue because the latest arriving message has already aged shall also be recorded.

### 2.9.7.0-4 Load Control PCSI/BM report PUCCH receive request skip (ID: 11197603)

**2.9.7.0-4.0-1**  (ID: `11197607`)

Load Control
Measurement: NR Cell Utilization

NetAct name: PUCCH\_REQ\_PCSI\_OR\_BM\_SKIP

PI ID: M55308CRD52005

Note: PM counter definitions can be found in NIDD as explained in 5G\_L2\_13022\_replaced([11192143](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_28iOyPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Update time:

When UlData\_PucchReceiveReq for PCSI/BM report is skipped due to PCSI reduction algorithm.

Increment value:

Increment 1 when one UlData\_PucchReceiveReq messages is skipped by the decision of 5G\_UP\_ALG\_29748\_replaced([11159309](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktI7kPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G\_L2\_22166\_replaced([11173150](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26ZgxPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Note: the counter is incremented for every UE's PCI/BM report proess request to L1 is skipped.

Load Control
Measurement: NR L2-PS overload report in NRCELLGRP

NetAct name: AVG\_MAXCS1\_SIZE\_DLSLT

PI ID: M55384CRD50017

Note: PM counter definitions can be found in NIDD as explained in 5G\_L2\_13022\_replaced([11192143](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_28iOyPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Update time:

* slots of type = loadTypeDlOnly (5G\_L2\_6333\_replaced([11212562](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2-mrUfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)));
* For performance reasons L2-PS can avoid updating the counter in the shared-memory bank (5G\_UP\_2732\_replaced([11392091](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ufcd8fvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) at each new sample. The samples can be accumulated locally and the counter updated once per radio frame (for example) with the accumulated value.

Increment value:

* The value which updates the counter is "cs1Size" calculated in 5G\_L2\_1853\_replaced([11212970](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2-pHlfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for the DL scheduler in slots categorized as "loadTypeDlOnly" (5G\_L2\_6333\_replaced([11212562](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2-mrUfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and multipled by 10. Reason for \*10: to represent the final average with 1-digit precision after the decimal point.

the counter is updated only if rdEnableOverloadControlCounters = 1.

### 2.9.7.0-7 Load Control Average of the maximum CS2 size as proposed by overload control (ID: 11197670)

---

**2.9.7.0-7.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11197676`)

Load Control
Measurement: NR L2-PS overload report in NRCELLGRP

NetAct name: AVG\_MAXCS2\_SIZE\_DLSLT

PI ID: M55384CRD50018

Note: PM counter definitions can be found in NIDD as explained in 5G\_L2\_13022\_replaced([11192143](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_28iOyPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Update time:

* slots of type = loadTypeDlOnly (5G\_L2\_6333\_replaced([11212562](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2-mrUfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)));
* For performance reasons L2-PS can avoid updating the counter in the shared-memory bank (5G\_UP\_2732\_replaced([11392091](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ufcd8fvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) at each new sample. The samples can be accumulated locally and the counter updated once per radio frame (for example) with the accumulated value.

Increment value:

For FR1:

* the value which updates the counter is "maxCs2Size" calculated in 5G\_L2\_7049\_replaced([11213344](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2-rj0_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for the DL scheduler in slots categorized as "loadTypeDlOnly" (5G\_L2\_6333\_replaced([11212562](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2-mrUfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

For FR2:

* if the CS2 list is reduced by the overload control, the value which updates the counter is "cs2Size" calculated in 5G\_L2\_7049\_replaced([11213344](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2-rj0_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for the DL scheduler, in slots categorized as "loadTypeDlOnly" (5G\_L2\_6333\_replaced([11212562](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2-mrUfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).
* if the CS2 list is not reduced by the overload control, the value which updates the counter is "cs1Size" calculated in 5G\_L2\_1853\_replaced([11212970](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2-pHlfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for the DL scheduler, in slots categorized as "loadTypeDlOnly" (5G\_L2\_6333\_replaced([11212562](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2-mrUfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

the counter is updated only if rdEnableOverloadControlCounters = 1.

### 2.9.7.0-8 Load Control Average of size of traversed CS2 list to create list of to-be-scheduled UEs (ID: 11197708)

**2.9.7.0-8.0-1**  (ID: `11197712`)

Load Control
Average of size of traversed CS2 list to create list of to-be-scheduled UEs  in slots categorized as loadTypeDlOnly

---

**2.9.7.0-8.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11197716`)

Load Control
Measurement: NR L2-PS overload report in NRCELLGRP

NetAct name: AVG\_CS2\_TRAVERSED\_SIZE\_DLSLT

PI ID: M55384CRD50019

Note: PM counter definitions can be found in NIDD as explained in 5G\_L2\_13022\_replaced([11192143](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_28iOyPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Update time:

* slots of type = loadTypeDlOnly (5G\_L2\_6333\_replaced([11212562](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2-mrUfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)));
* For performance reasons L2-PS can avoid updating the counter in the shared-memory bank (5G\_UP\_2732\_replaced([11392091](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ufcd8fvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) at each new sample. The samples can be accumulated locally and the counter updated once per radio frame (for example) with the accumulated value.

Increment value:

In FR1, the DL td-scheduler goes through the UEs in cs2 list, starting from the one with highest priority. If a UE satisfies all the checks specified in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4297\_replaced([11177362](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuu3A_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), it is put into the to-be-scheduled-ue list, otherwise that UE is discarted and the td-sch will check the next one in the cs2 list, until any condition to stops this selection kicks-in. Requirement 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4293\_replaced([11177286](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuuP-fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) provides an overview of this selection.

The value which updates the counter is the total number of Ues from the cs2 list that the td-sch goes through in the process of creating the to-be-scheduled-ue list, for slots which are categorized as "loadTypeDlOnly" (5G\_L2\_6333\_replaced([11212562](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2-mrUfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

In FR1 TDD the "Beam based" DL MU-MIMO (5G\_UP\_ALG\_4358\_replaced([11141887](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kr17FfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) is obsolete and replaced by the "Virtual UE based" DL MU-MIMO (5G\_UP\_ALG\_7427\_replaced([11142415](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kr4XVfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). This means that it's not needed to handle multiple CS2 lists created by the SD scheduler.

In FR2 one single td-sch goes through several cs2 lists (one for each component carrier handled by the td-sch) and selects Ues to build the respective to-be-scheduled-ue lists as specified in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4293\_replaced([11177286](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuuP-fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))/5G\_UP\_ALG\_4295\_replaced([11177311](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuuP_vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). The value which updates the counter is given by:

SUM(all DL carriers J handled by same DL td-scheduler){ number of UEs from CS2 list of carrier J the UL td-sch goes through in the process of creating the to-be-scheduled-ue list for carrier J, in a slot categorized as "loadTypeDlOnly"}

Reason: the aggregation dimension of this counter is the NRCELLGRP and in FR2 one NRCELLGRP includes multiple cells/carriers handled by the same td-scheduler. The scope of this counter is to give an indication of how 'expensive' this procedure (of building the to-be-scheduled-ue list) is for the scheduler, therefore also the aggregated value over multiple CS2 list is an helpful metric.

the counter is updated only if rdEnableOverloadControlCounters = 1.

---

**2.9.7.0-8.0-2**  (ID: `11197720`)

Load Control
Average of size of traversed CS2 list to create list of to-be-scheduled UEs  in slots categorized as loadTypeUlOnly

---

**2.9.7.0-8.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11197724`)

Load Control
Measurement: NR L2-PS overload report in NRCELLGRP

NetAct name: AVG\_CS2\_TRAVERSED\_SIZE\_ULSLT

PI ID: M55384CRD50055

Note: PM counter definitions can be found in NIDD as explained in 5G\_L2\_13022\_replaced([11192143](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_28iOyPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Update time:

* slots of type = loadTypeUlOnly (5G\_L2\_6333\_replaced([11212562](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2-mrUfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)));
* For performance reasons L2-PS can avoid updating the counter in the shared-memory bank (5G\_UP\_2732\_replaced([11392091](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ufcd8fvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) at each new sample. The samples can be accumulated locally and the counter updated once per radio frame (for example) with the accumulated value.

Increment value:

In FR1, the UL td-scheduler goes through the UEs in cs2 list, starting from the one with highest priority. If a UE satisfies all the checks specified in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4871\_replaced([11196450](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwCepPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), it is put into the to-be-scheduled-ue list, otherwise that UE is discarted and the td-sch will check the next one in the cs2 list, until any condition to stops this selection kicks-in.

The value which updates the counter is the total number of Ues from the cs2 list that the td-sch goes through in the process of creating the to-be-scheduled-ue list, for slots which are categorized as "loadTypeUlOnly" (5G\_L2\_6333\_replaced([11212562](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2-mrUfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

In FR2 one single td-sch goes through several cs2 lists (one for each component carrier handled by the td-sch) and selects Ues to build the respective to-be-scheduled-ue lists. The value which updates the counter is given by:

SUM(all UL carriers J handled by same UL td-scheduler){ number of UEs from CS2 list of carrier J the UL td-sch goes through in the process of creating the to-be-scheduled-ue list for carrier J, in a slot categorized as "loadTypeUlOnly"}

Reason: the aggregation dimension of this counter is the NRCELLGRP and in FR2 one NRCELLGRP includes multiple cells/carriers handled by the same td-scheduler. The scope of this counter is to give an indication of how 'expensive' this procedure (of building the to-be-scheduled-ue list) is for the scheduler, therefore also the aggregated value over multiple CS2 list is an helpful metric.

the counter is updated only if rdEnableOverloadControlCounters = 1.

---

**2.9.7.0-8.0-3**  (ID: `11197729`)

Load Control
Average of size of traversed CS2 list to create list of to-be-scheduled UEs  for DL in slots categorized as loadTypeMixed

The samples can be accumulated locally and the counter updated once per radio frame (for example) with the accumulated value.

### 2.9.7.0-9 Load Control Average of rrmMaxNumUesFdmOlc as proposed by overload control (ID: 11197746)

---

**2.9.7.0-9.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11197755`)

Load Control
Measurement: NR L2-PS overload report in NRCELLGRP

NetAct name: AVG\_MAX\_FDM\_UE\_OLC\_DLSLT

PI ID: M55384CRD50020

Note: PM counter definitions can be found in NIDD as explained in 5G\_L2\_13022\_replaced([11192143](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_28iOyPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Update time:

* slots of type = loadTypeDlOnly (5G\_L2\_6333\_replaced([11212562](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2-mrUfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)));
* For performance reasons L2-PS can avoid updating the counter in the shared-memory bank (5G\_UP\_2732\_replaced([11392091](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ufcd8fvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) at each new sample. The samples can be accumulated locally and the counter updated once per radio frame (for example) with the accumulated value.

Increment value:

In FR1, the value which updates the counter is "rrmMaxNumUesFdmOlc" calculated in 5G\_L2\_7048\_replaced([11213384](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2-rj2vvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for the DL scheduler in slots categorized as "loadTypeDlOnly" (5G\_L2\_6333\_replaced([11212562](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2-mrUfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

In FR2 there's no FDM and "rrmMaxNumUesFdmOlc" is set to a constant value by L2-PS overload control. The counter is still updated by "rrmMaxNumUesFdmOlc" value for the DL scheduler in slots categorized as "loadTypeDlOnly" (5G\_L2\_6333\_replaced([11212562](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2-mrUfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

the counter is updated only if rdEnableOverloadControlCounters = 1.

**2.9.7.0-10.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11197790`)

Load Control
**Measurement: NR L2 overload report in DU for RLC and MAC**

**NetAct name: AVG\_FDM\_UE\_DLSLT**

**PI ID: M55381CRD50003**

**Note: PM counter definitions can be found in NIDD as explained in 5G\_L2\_13022\_replaced([11192143](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_28iOyPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).**

**Update time:**

* slots of type = loadTypeDlOnly (5G\_L2\_6333\_replaced([11212562](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2-mrUfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)));
* For performance reasons L2-PS can avoid updating the counter in the shared-memory bank (5G\_UP\_2732\_replaced([11392091](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ufcd8fvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) at each new sample. The samples can be accumulated locally and the counter updated once per radio frame (for example) with the accumulated value.

**Increment value:**

**- There are two legacy counters which count the number of scheduled UEs in DL: ACC\_SCHED\_UE\_PDSCH\_NOPAGING and ACC\_SCHED\_UE\_PDSCH which respectively exclude and don't exclude Paging. AVG\_FDM\_UE\_DLSLT is updated with same value as reported into dlLoadControlData.CsListInfo.scheduledUes.**

the counter is updated only if rdEnableOverloadControlCounters = 1.

### 2.9.7.0-11 Load Control Average time available for scheduling-related processing (ID: 11197818)

**2.9.7.0-11.0-1**  (ID: `11197822`)

Load Control
Average time available for scheduling-related processing in slots categorized as loadTypeDlOnly

---

**2.9.7.0-11.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11197827`)

Load Control
Measurement: NR L2-PS overload report in NRCELLGRP

NetAct name: AVG\_AVL\_PROC\_TIME\_SCHD\_DLSLT

PI ID: M55384CRD50009

Note: PM counter definitions can be found in NIDD as explained in 5G\_L2\_13022\_replaced([11192143](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_28iOyPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Update time:

* slots of type = loadTypeDlOnly
* For performance reasons L2-PS can avoid updating the counter in the shared-memory bank (5G\_UP\_2732\_replaced([11392091](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ufcd8fvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) at each new sample. The samples can be accumulated locally and the counter updated once per radio frame (for example) with the accumulated value.

Increment value:

For a slot n of type = loadTypeDlOnly, the time available to the DL-scheduler X for scheduling-related processing is calculated as:

availTimeForSchedProcessing(n, DL-sch X) = slotAwareOptimalRatio(n, DL-sch X) \* ( slotDuration - slotDelay(n) )

where:

* slotAwareOptimalRatio is calculated in 5G\_L2\_6333\_replaced([11212562](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2-mrUfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and adjusted in 5G\_L2\_5845\_replaced([11213879](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2-vONfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) (if Load Balancing between schedulers applies);
* slotDuration is the duration of the slot in the radio frame;
* slotDelay is the initial slot delay for the current slot n, see 5G\_L2\_1863\_replaced([11213446](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2-sK5vvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ));

the counter is updated only if rdEnableOverloadControlCounters = 1.

---

**2.9.7.0-11.0-2**  (ID: `11197831`)

Load Control
Average time available for scheduling-related processing in slots categorized as loadTypeUlOnly

---

**2.9.7.0-11.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11197834`)

Load Control
Measurement: NR L2-PS overload report in NRCELLGRP

NetAct name: AVG\_AVL\_PROC\_TIME\_SCHD\_ULSLT

PI ID: M55384CRD50045

Note: PM counter definitions can be found in NIDD as explained in 5G\_L2\_13022\_replaced([11192143](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_28iOyPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Update time:

* slots of type = loadTypeUlOnly
* For performance reasons L2-PS can avoid updating the counter in the shared-memory bank (5G\_UP\_2732\_replaced([11392091](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ufcd8fvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) at each new sample. The samples can be accumulated locally and the counter updated once per radio frame (for example) with the accumulated value.

Increment value:

For a slot n of type = loadTypeUlOnly, the time available to the UL-scheduler X for scheduling-related processing is calculated as:

availTimeForSchedProcessing(n, UL-sch X) = slotAwareOptimalRatio(n, UL-sch X) \* ( slotDuration - slotDelay(n) )

where:

* slotAwareOptimalRatio is calculated in 5G\_L2\_6333\_replaced([11212562](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2-mrUfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and adjusted in 5G\_L2\_5845\_replaced([11213879](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2-vONfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) (if Load Balancing between schedulers applies);
* slotDuration is the duration of the slot in the radio frame;
* slotDelay is the initial slot delay for the current slot n, see 5G\_L2\_1863\_replaced([11213446](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2-sK5vvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ));

the counter is updated only if rdEnableOverloadControlCounters = 1.

---

Load Control
Average time available for DL scheduling-related processing in slots categorized as loadTypeMixed

---

* slotDuration is the duration of the slot in the radio frame;

### 2.9.7.0-13 Load Control Average time consumed for scheduling-related processing (ID: 11197894)

**2.9.7.0-13.0-1**  (ID: `11197898`)

Load Control
Average time consumed for scheduling-related processing in slots categorized as loadTypeDlOnly

---

**2.9.7.0-13.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11197903`)

Load Control
Measurement: NR L2-PS overload report in NRCELLGRP

NetAct name: AVG\_CONS\_PROC\_TIME\_SCHD\_DLSLT

PI ID: M55384CRD50011

Note: PM counter definitions can be found in NIDD as explained in 5G\_L2\_13022\_replaced([11192143](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_28iOyPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Update time:

* slots of type = loadTypeDlOnly
* For performance reasons L2-PS can avoid updating the counter in the shared-memory bank (5G\_UP\_2732\_replaced([11392091](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ufcd8fvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) at each new sample. The samples can be accumulated locally and the counter updated once per radio frame (for example) with the accumulated value.

Increment value:

In order to calculate the processing time taken by a scheduler, measurements which are already exisiting in L2-PS code will be reused. They measure purely:

* pre-scheduler + td-scheduler time (let's name it preTdSchTime here);
* fd-scheduler time (let's name it fdSchTime here);
* post-scheduler time (let's name it postSchTime here);

Some real time processing outside these specific functions is not measured, causing an underestimation of the processing time in the order of some percent points.

For a slot n of type = loadTypeDlOnly, the time consumed by the DL-scheduler X for scheduling-related processing is calculated as:

consumedTimeForSchedProcessing(n, dl-sch X) = preTdSchTime(n, dl-sch X) + fdSchTime(n, dl-sch X) + postSchTime(n, dl-sch X)

the counter is updated only if rdEnableOverloadControlCounters = 1.

---

**2.9.7.0-13.0-2**  (ID: `11197907`)

Load Control
Average time consumed for scheduling-related processing in slots categorized as loadTypeUlOnly

---

### 2.9.7.0-15 Load Control Average time available for non-scheduling-related processing (ID: 11197967)

**2.9.7.0-15.0-1**  (ID: `11197972`)

Load Control
Introduction

---

**2.9.7.0-15.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11197975`)

Load Control
The L2-PS load control manages the time for non scheduling-related processing per core and not per DL/UL scheduler, therefore also these counters shall measure the time for non scheduling-related processing which is globally available on a specific core.

“Core” is not a valid ‘aggregation dimension’ for PM counters and “NRCELLGRP” is chosen instead. Nevertheless, for the reason mentioned above, the measured time doesn’t refer to the non-scheduling processing of the NRCELLGRP but of the core where the NRCELLGRP is deployed, according to the table below:

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Counter | Update Time | Update Value |
| AVG\_AVL\_PROC\_TIME\_NSCHD\_DLSLT (NRCELLGRP A) | Slot of load type = loadTypeDlOnly | Time available for non scheduling-related processing on the L2-PS core hosting the DL-sch of NRCELLGRP A |
| AVG\_AVL\_PROC\_TIME\_NSCHD\_ULSLT (NRCELLGRP A) | Slot of load type type = loadTypeUlOnly | Time available for non scheduling-related processing on the L2-PS core hosting the UL-sch of NRCELLGRP A |
| AVG\_AVL\_PROC\_TIME\_NSCHD\_MIXSLT\_DL (NRCELLGRP A) | Slot of load type = loadTypeMixed | Time available for non scheduling-related processing on the L2-PS core hosting the DL-sch of NRCELLGRP A |
| AVG\_AVL\_PROC\_TIME\_NSCHD\_MIXSLT\_UL (NRCELLGRP A) | Slot of load type = loadTypeMixed | Time available for non scheduling-related processing on the L2-PS core hosting the UL-sch of NRCELLGRP A |

The calculation of the “Time available for non scheduler-related processing” is done per core and slot Load type and the same value is used to update the affected counters for that slot load type.

For a slot n of load type = loadTypeDlOnly and a core Y:

* Calculated Value:

availTimeForNonSchedProcessing(n, core Y) = slotDuration - slotDelay(n) - SUM(all DL-schedulers X on core Y){ consumedTimeForSchedProcessing(n, dl-sch X) }

where:

* consumedTimeForSchedProcessing(n, dl-sch X) is calculated in 5G\_L2\_22676\_replaced([11197898](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_29ROl_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ));
* Affected Counters:
* AVG\_AVL\_PROC\_TIME\_NSCHD\_DLSLT of all the NRCELLGRPs which DL-scheduler is hosted by core Y, are updated with the value above;

For a slot n of load type = loadTypeUlOnly and a core Y:

* Calculated Value:

availTimeForNonSchedProcessing(n, core Y) = slotDuration - slotDelay(n) - SUM(all UL-schedulers X on core Y){ consumedTimeForSchedProcessing(n, ul-sch X) }

where:

* consumedTimeForSchedProcessing(n, ul-sch X) is calculated in 5G\_L2\_22715\_replaced([11197907](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_29ROmfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ));
* Affected Counters:
* AVG\_AVL\_PROC\_TIME\_NSCHD\_ULSLT of all the NRCELLGRPs which UL-scheduler is hosted by core Y, are updated with the value above;

For a slot n of load type = loadTypeMixed and a core Y:

* Calculated Value:

availTimeForNonSchedProcessing(n, core Y) = slotDuration - slotDelay(n) - SUM(all DL-schedulers X on core Y){ consumedTimeForSchedProcessing(n, dl-sch X) - SUM(all UL-schedulers X on core Y){ consumedTimeForSchedProcessing(n, ul-sch X) }

where:

* consumedTimeForSchedProcessing(n, dl-sch X) is calculated in 5G\_L2\_22717\_replaced([11197916](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_29ROm_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ));
* consumedTimeForSchedProcessing(n, ul-sch X) is calculated in 5G\_L2\_22719\_replaced([11197924](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_29ROnfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ));
* Affected Counters:
* AVG\_AVL\_PROC\_TIME\_NSCHD\_ MIXSLT\_DL of all the NRCELLGRPs which DL-scheduler is hosted by core Y, are updated with the value above;
* AVG\_AVL\_PROC\_TIME\_NSCHD\_ MIXSLT\_UL of all the NRCELLGRPs which UL-scheduler is hosted by core Y, are updated with the value above;
* slotDuration is the duration of the slot in the radio frame;
* slotDelay is the initial slot delay for the current slot n, see 5G\_L2\_1863\_replaced([11213446](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2-sK5vvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ));

Note:

Based on this definition, depending on the deployment of the schedulers on the L2-PS cores, some of the counters shall provide identical information:

Slot of load type = loadTypeDlOnly:

* AVG\_AVL\_PROC\_TIME\_NSCHD\_DLSLT (NRCELLGRP A) == AVG\_AVL\_PROC\_TIME\_NSCHD\_DLSLT (NRCELLGRP B), if DL-sch(NRCELLGRP A) and DL-sch(NRCELLGRP B) are deployed on same L2-PS core;

Slot of load type = loadTypeUlOnly:

* AVG\_AVL\_PROC\_TIME\_NSCHD\_ULSLT (NRCELLGRP A) == AVG\_AVL\_PROC\_TIME\_NSCHD\_ULSLT (NRCELLGRP B), if UL-sch(NRCELLGRP A) and UL-sch(NRCELLGRP B) are deployed on same L2-PS core;

Slot of load type = loadTypeMixed:

* AVG\_AVL\_PROC\_TIME\_NSCHD\_MIXSLT\_DL (NRCELLGRP A) == AVG\_AVL\_PROC\_TIME\_NSCHD\_MIXSLT\_DL (NRCELLGRP B), if DL-sch(NRCELLGRP A) and DL-sch(NRCELLGRP B) are deployed on same L2-PS core;
* AVG\_AVL\_PROC\_TIME\_NSCHD\_MIXSLT\_UL (NRCELLGRP A) == AVG\_AVL\_PROC\_TIME\_NSCHD\_MIXSLT\_UL (NRCELLGRP B), if UL-sch(NRCELLGRP A) and UL-sch(NRCELLGRP B) are deployed on same L2-PS core;
* AVG\_AVL\_PROC\_TIME\_NSCHD\_MIXSLT\_DL == AVG\_AVL\_PROC\_TIME\_NSCHD\_MIXSLT\_UL, if DL-sch and UL-sch of same or different NRCELLGRPs are deployed on same L2-PS core;

**2.9.7.0-15.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11197983`)

Load Control
Measurement: NR L2-PS overload report in NRCELLGRP

NetAct name: AVG\_AVL\_PROC\_TIME\_NSCHD\_DLSLT

PI ID: M55384CRD50013

Note: PM counter definitions can be found in NIDD as explained in 5G\_L2\_13022\_replaced([11192143](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_28iOyPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Update time:

* slots of type = loadTypeDlOnly
* For performance reasons L2-PS can avoid updating the counter in the shared-memory bank (5G\_UP\_2732\_replaced([11392091](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ufcd8fvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) at each new sample. The samples can be accumulated locally and the counter updated once per radio frame (for example) with the accumulated value.

Increment value:

* see 5G\_L2\_22909\_replaced([11197972](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_29R1rPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ));

The counter is updated only if rdEnableOverloadControlCounters = 1.
